app.long-description: This app is intended for people with PKU. It combines a scale, nutritional table, calculator, pencil and paper.
app.auth-error: Unfortunately this did not work. Try it again. Always use the same login method.
app.limit: Entries limit reached. Support with a subscription for unlimited entries. See settings for more.

common.export-description: Your data will be exported as a CSV file.

home.title: Home
home.signin-desc: Easily sign in on any device. Data syncs in real time.
home.install-desc: Can be installed on any device (Android, iOS, Mac, Windows).
home.phe-anywhere-desc: AI, search, scanner and calculator — four ways to determine the phenylalanine of any food.
home.weight-photo-desc: No need to weigh — let the AI estimate the weight straight from a photo.
home.diet-mgmt-desc: Logging is lightning fast — with suggestions and automatic food icons.
home.insights-desc: Diary, diet report and blood values — all your PKU data clearly in one place.
home.compare: One app, not a whole kit
home.compare-desc: What used to be a hassle now takes just a few clicks.
home.about-description: From the developer of one of the first PKU apps for Android "Phe Rechner", as well as "PKU Manager". Likewise introduction of the barcode scanner and AI calculator specifically for PKU. Development of PKU apps since 2013.
home.trust-independent-desc: By and for people with PKU.

faq.title: Frequently asked questions
faq.a1: PKU Tools is an all-in-one nutrition app for people with phenylketonuria (PKU). It replaces the scale, nutrition table, calculator and pen-and-paper with a single app for a low-phenylalanine (Phe) diet.
faq.a2: Yes. The food search, barcode scanner and Phe calculator are completely free and don't even need an account. The diary, blood values, own foods and the AI calculator come as a free trial, with no ads. Optional Premium subscriptions raise these limits and add CSV export and more AI.
faq.q3: Which devices can I use it on?
faq.a3: PKU Tools is a progressive web app. It installs on Android, iOS, Mac and Windows and is also on the Google Play Store. Your data syncs across your devices in real time.
faq.q4: Is my health data private and secure?
faq.a4: Yes. Your data is stored securely and is not sold. It stays yours, and you can export or delete it anytime. PKU Tools is open source and independent, built by and for the PKU community. See the Privacy Policy for details.
faq.a5: For the basic tools — food search, barcode scanner and the Phe calculator — you don't need an account. An account is needed to save your data (diary, blood values, own foods) and for the AI calculator. Signing in also syncs everything across your devices.
faq.more: More questions? Visit the Help section

help.title: Help
help.install-p1: This web app can be installed on any device like an app.

help.install-p4: Click the little installation icon in the address bar in Chrome, Brave or Edge.

faq.title: Frequently asked questions
faq.a1: PKU Tools is an all-in-one nutrition app for people with phenylketonuria (PKU). It combines the scale, nutrition table, calculator and pen-and-paper in one place for a low-phenylalanine (Phe) diet. It supports your everyday diet management but doesn't replace the advice of your metabolic clinic or dietitian. See the Disclaimer for details.
faq.a2: For the basic tools — food search, barcode scanner and the Phe calculator — you don't need an account. An account is needed to save your data (diary, blood values, own foods) and for the AI calculator. Once you sign in, your data also syncs across your devices in real time.
faq.q3: How does the AI estimate weight and nutritional values?
faq.a3: You describe the food in a few words or take a photo. The AI then estimates the portion weight, phenylalanine and calories, so you can skip the scale and the lookup. These are estimates, so always double-check them. Please don't share sensitive personal data, including in photos. The AI model is Google Gemini.
faq.q4: The AI recognised the food incorrectly — how do I fix it?
faq.a4: The food name and the short explanation show what the AI recognised. If that's wrong, tap "Correct" and describe what the food actually is. It then re-estimates.
faq.q5: Where does the food data come from?
faq.a5: Food Search uses the USDA FoodData Central database (fdc.nal.usda.gov), which Google also draws on for the nutrition facts in its search results. The barcode scanner uses Open Food Facts (openfoodfacts.org). Your own foods also appear in the search, and when you're signed in so do foods shared by the community.
faq.q6: The barcode scanner or camera isn't working — what can I do?
faq.a6: First check the camera permission. On the web, allow the camera for your browser. On Android, long-press the app icon and open "Site settings". If the barcode isn't recognised, switch the camera with the camera selector — sometimes the wrong one is selected and shows the barcode blurry. If it still doesn't work, restart the app and your device. If a product isn't found, it may not be in Open Food Facts yet. Then enter the protein from the label in the Phe Calculator, or use the AI Calculator to read the whole nutrition label.
faq.q7: How is protein converted to Phe?
faq.a7: Phe is calculated from protein: Phe (mg) = protein (g) × factor. As a rule of thumb, 1 g of protein is about 50 mg Phe. For a closer result the app uses a factor per food type: 27 for fruit, 35 for vegetables, 46 for meat and 50 for other foods (mg Phe per gram of protein). Pick the food type in the Phe Calculator and enter the protein. The barcode scanner works the same way.
faq.q8: How do I log a food?
faq.a8: Tap the + button in the navigation bar. It opens your preferred tool: AI, Search, Scanner or the Phe Calculator (choose it in Settings). Calculate your food and tap Add to save it to today's diary. The diary also shows suggestions based on what you've logged before, which you can add with one tap.
faq.q9: How do I keep track of my Phe intake over time?
faq.a9: The Diet Report shows your Phe intake over time as a chart, with a daily view and a trend view (7-day average). An averages card lets you pick a period and shows your average Phe and calories as a share of your daily target. You can mark incomplete days, view your entries in a table, and export everything to CSV.
faq.q10: How do I track and chart my blood values?
faq.a10: In Blood Values, add your lab results for phenylalanine and optionally tyrosine with the date. They appear on an interactive chart. Set your target range to see which readings fall inside it. You can export the data as CSV, SVG or PNG and change the unit in Settings.
faq.q11: Can I add and share my own foods?
faq.a11: Yes. Under Own Food you can save custom foods for quick adding, and they appear in your Food Search. You can also share a food with the PKU community so other users can find it.
faq.q12: How do I set my Phe tolerance and daily target?
faq.a12: Set your daily Phe tolerance and your calorie needs in Settings. The diary then shows your progress as bars or rings, and you can switch the style in Settings.
faq.q13: Can I change the language and appearance?
faq.a13: You can switch the language (English, German, Spanish, French) using the language picker in the app. The appearance — device theme, light or dark — is set in Settings.
faq.q14: How does the Premium subscription work?
faq.a14: The free trial version has fixed limits, and Premium subscriptions raise them, depending on the plan. To subscribe, open Settings and tap "Join" on a plan. You complete the subscription on Ko-fi and receive a license key, which you enter and activate in Settings. You can cancel anytime via Ko-fi or your PayPal account.
faq.q15: Is my health data private and secure?
faq.a15: Yes. Your data is stored securely and is not sold or shared for advertising. When you start, you give explicit consent to health-data processing. You can revoke it or delete your data anytime in Settings. See the Privacy Policy for details.
faq.q-contact: How do I get help or send feedback?
faq.q-install: How do I install PKU Tools?
